subject: Physics
title: Ohm's Law: V = IR
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Ohm's Law: V = IR.

hook.text: Georg Ohm discovered the simple law governing electricity: voltage equals current times resistance.

buildup.text: Voltage is the push, current is the flow, and resistance is what opposes it. Three quantities, one equation.

discovery.text: Double the voltage and you double the current—as long as resistance stays the same.

twist.text: Ohm's colleagues rejected his work for years. He lost his teaching job over the controversy.

climax.text: Today, Ohm's law is the first equation every electrical engineer learns. The unit of resistance is the ohm.

punchline.text: V = IR. Three letters. The foundation of every circuit.

quiz.question: According to Ohm's law, what happens if you double the voltage across a resistor?
